title: Q & A Session | MANAGING ANGER | HG AMITASANA DAS
speaker: HG Amitasana Dasa

So, we have some more questions which have come in today.
The question is from Venkat Sandeep.
Question is, sometimes I procrastinate about a situation and I get angry.
Please provide a practical solution for such situations.
To a large extent, when we practice the principles of Krishna Consciousness, which is following
the four regulative principles and chanting the Hare Krishna Maha Mantra, we get the required
strength to handle such situations in life, like for example, laziness, procrastination,
Losing our temper for small, small things.
So I would strongly recommend, see if you can try improving in practice of the principles
of Krishna Consciousness more sincerely, more rigidly.
This question is from Nikita Nandini, kindly give insight, what is free will?
Will it affect as if we go by our free will?
Free will means we all have ability to choose.
We are not like robots where we are helplessly made to act.
Like robot, you just program in a particular way and then it just helplessly acts.
Unlike that, the Lord has given us a free will.
We have a choice, although we don't have complete independence, but yes, we have independence
within some framework, within some limits.
For example, today we had a choice.
We had a choice to attend this Gita live session or not to attend it.
We could have taken a little more rest, an afternoon nap on Sunday afternoon.
So Krishna respects that free will.
He has given us that free will.
Otherwise, if He just imposes, forces us to act in such a way, then there is no question
There is no question of bhakti.
And that sharp spiritual intelligence comes when we are regularly doing satsang, when we are regularly reading transcendental literatures like Bhagavad Gita, Bhagavatam, books of Srila Prabhupada.
Then we will have the spiritual intelligence, we will have the right discrimination and we will be able to use our free will properly.
This question is from Archana Singh. If I kill an ant, so I will get a reaction, but that ant was destined to be killed by me, so why do I have to suffer?
Actually it is not true that when I kill an ant, the ant was destined to be killed by me. No.
Yes, it's a combination of two things. The law of karma is very intricate.
It is quite possible that the person who is recipient of our act is getting due what is due to him or her as per the law of karma.
But quite possible I also have an independent desire to act in a particular way.
For example, I am very short tempered and I act recklessly. I am not at all compassionate.
God can bring together these two things.
For example, let's say a person murders someone. Recently we had this UP gangster, Dubey.
Now somebody may say that it was the destiny of those police people to get killed, so why do we blame this gangster, Dubey?
So here two factors come in play. One is that yes, somebody is destined to get karma in a particular way, the reaction to his karmic deeds in a particular way.
And quite possible that person because of his misuse of free will has some desire to act in some wrong ways.
The Lord can mystically bring together these two things.
On one side, the person's karmic reaction is getting squared off.
The other side, a person can be creating new karma.
So in this particular situation, if we indiscriminately kill animals, we cannot justify our act saying that
it is the destiny of the animals.
Quite possible we might be creating fresh karma for ourselves which can be detrimental for us.
So we have to be careful.
All unnecessary killing has to be avoided.
In this world, we cannot make all killing zero.
Even when we are walking, at times we will be killing so many and so many things without our knowledge.
But all unnecessary killing, all indiscriminate killing, we can try to avoid.
This question is from Shobha Jaiswal.

if we are experiencing a higher taste,
then we will not get irritated by small, small things.

and somebody comes and takes five rupees from that person,
he will get irritated a lot.

and somebody comes and takes five rupees.

when we are in control of our mind,
we are experiencing blissful reciprocation of the Lord,
then so-called small, small situations which life throws on us,
